title: Sterculia schumanniana
intro: Sterculia schumanniana, also known as the Sterculia tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Sterculia schumanniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as to support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Sterculia schumanniana is classified in the family Malvaceae and order Malvales. It belongs to the genus Sterculia. The family Malvaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. Sterculia schumanniana is native to the tropical regions of Africa and Madagascar.
